# chronosphere_log_ingest_config (Resource)

Singleton per-tenant parser configuration controlling how raw log payloads are parsed into structured fields and how timestamps, severities, services, and other fields are normalized.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "chronosphere_log_ingest_config" "config" {
  # Parse raw JSON log lines into structured fields.
  field_parser {
    mode = "ENABLED"

    source {
      selector = "raw_message"
    }

    parser {
      parser_type = "JSON"
    }
  }

  # Normalize common fields across heterogeneous log sources.
  field_normalization {
    timestamp {
      source {
        selector = "timestamp"
      }
      source {
        selector = "ts"
      }
      source {
        selector = "@timestamp"
      }
    }

    service {
      source {
        selector = "service"
      }
      source {
        selector = "app"
      }
      default_value = "UNKNOWN"
    }

    severity {
      source {
        selector = "level"
      }
      source {
        selector = "severity"
      }
      value_map = {
        "debug" = "DEBUG"
        "info"  = "INFO"
        "warn"  = "WARNING"
        "error" = "ERROR"
        "fatal" = "CRITICAL"
      }
      default_value = "INFO"
    }

    message {
      source {
        selector = "message"
      }
      source {
        selector = "msg"
      }
      default_value = "no message"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `field_normalization` (Block List, Max: 1) Field normalization rules that map and standardize well-known fields (timestamp, severity, message, service) across log formats. Runs after parsing. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization))
- `field_parser` (Block List) Parsers applied to specific fields within structured logs (or to fields produced by a plaintext parser). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser))
- `plaintext_parser` (Block List) Parsers applied to plaintext logs as they enter the ingest pipeline. The first parser that matches a log is used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--plaintext_parser))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization`

Optional:

- `custom_field_normalization` (Block List) Normalization rules for additional custom fields. These fields are not indexed; use them for things like environment, region, or user ID. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--custom_field_normalization))
- `message` (Block List, Max: 1) Rule that extracts and transforms a string value from a log field, with optional regex sanitization, default value, and value mapping. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--message))
- `service` (Block List, Max: 1) Rule that extracts and transforms a string value from a log field, with optional regex sanitization, default value, and value mapping. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--service))
- `severity` (Block List, Max: 1) Rule that extracts and transforms a string value from a log field, with optional regex sanitization, default value, and value mapping. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--severity))
- `timestamp` (Block List, Max: 1) Normalization rule for the well-known `timestamp` field. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--timestamp))

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--custom_field_normalization"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.custom_field_normalization`

Optional:

- `normalization` (Block List, Max: 1) Rule that extracts and transforms a string value from a log field, with optional regex sanitization, default value, and value mapping. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--custom_field_normalization--normalization))
- `target` (String) Name of the target field where the normalized value is stored.

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--custom_field_normalization--normalization"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.custom_field_normalization.normalization`

Optional:

- `default_value` (String) Value to use when no source field contains a value.
- `sanitize_patterns` (List of String) Regex patterns used to extract and sanitize the value. Each pattern must have exactly one capturing group, whose contents are used as the result.
- `source` (Block List) Ordered list of field paths to check for values. The first non-empty value found is used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--custom_field_normalization--normalization--source))
- `value_map` (Map of String) Optional mapping that normalizes raw values to canonical ones (for example, `warn` to `WARNING`).

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--custom_field_normalization--normalization--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.custom_field_normalization.normalization.source`

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.




<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--message"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.message`

Optional:

- `default_value` (String) Value to use when no source field contains a value.
- `sanitize_patterns` (List of String) Regex patterns used to extract and sanitize the value. Each pattern must have exactly one capturing group, whose contents are used as the result.
- `source` (Block List) Ordered list of field paths to check for values. The first non-empty value found is used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--message--source))
- `value_map` (Map of String) Optional mapping that normalizes raw values to canonical ones (for example, `warn` to `WARNING`).

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--message--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.message.source`

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.

Optional:

- `default_value` (String) Value to use when no source field contains a value.
- `sanitize_patterns` (List of String) Regex patterns used to extract and sanitize the value. Each pattern must have exactly one capturing group, whose contents are used as the result.
- `source` (Block List) Ordered list of field paths to check for values. The first non-empty value found is used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--service--source))
- `value_map` (Map of String) Optional mapping that normalizes raw values to canonical ones (for example, `warn` to `WARNING`).

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--service--source"></a>

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--severity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.severity`

Optional:

- `default_value` (String) Value to use when no source field contains a value.
- `sanitize_patterns` (List of String) Regex patterns used to extract and sanitize the value. Each pattern must have exactly one capturing group, whose contents are used as the result.
- `source` (Block List) Ordered list of field paths to check for values. The first non-empty value found is used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--severity--source))
- `value_map` (Map of String) Optional mapping that normalizes raw values to canonical ones (for example, `warn` to `WARNING`).

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--severity--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.severity.source`

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--timestamp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.timestamp`

Optional:

- `source` (Block List) Ordered list of field paths to check for timestamp values. The first non-empty value found is used. Common sources include `timestamp`, `@timestamp`, `time`, and `datetime`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_normalization--timestamp--source))

<a id="nestedblock--field_normalization--timestamp--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_normalization.timestamp.source`

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.




<a id="nestedblock--field_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser`

Required:

- `parser` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Parser configuration. Exactly one of `regex_parser`, `key_value_parser`, or `grok_parser` must be set, matching `parser_type`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser--parser))
- `source` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Path of the field to parse. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser--source))

Optional:

- `destination` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to write the parsed output to. If omitted, parsed fields are written at the root. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser--destination))
- `mode` (String) Mode that controls when the field parser runs on incoming logs.

<a id="nestedblock--field_parser--parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser.parser`

Required:

- `parser_type` (String) Type of parser to apply. Determines which of `regex_parser`, `key_value_parser`, or `grok_parser` must be set.

Optional:

- `grok_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Grok parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `GROK`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser--parser--grok_parser))
- `key_value_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Key/value parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `KEY_VALUE`. Duplicate keys keep the first occurrence. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser--parser--key_value_parser))
- `regex_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Regex parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `REGEX`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--field_parser--parser--regex_parser))

<a id="nestedblock--field_parser--parser--grok_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser.parser.grok_parser`

Required:

- `pattern` (String) Grok pattern to apply. Named capture groups become named fields in the extracted log.


<a id="nestedblock--field_parser--parser--key_value_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser.parser.key_value_parser`

Required:

- `delimiter` (String) String used to split the input into individual key/value pairs.
- `pair_separator` (String) String used to split each pair into a key and value.

Optional:

- `trim_set` (String) Unicode code points to trim from the beginning and end of each key and value.


<a id="nestedblock--field_parser--parser--regex_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser.parser.regex_parser`

Required:

- `regex` (String) RE2 regular expression pattern. Named capturing groups become named fields in the extracted log.



<a id="nestedblock--field_parser--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser.source`

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.


<a id="nestedblock--field_parser--destination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `field_parser.destination`

Required:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--plaintext_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `plaintext_parser`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the parser. Must be unique within the configuration.
- `parser` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Parser configuration. Exactly one of `regex_parser`, `key_value_parser`, or `grok_parser` must be set, matching `parser_type`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser))

Optional:

- `keep_original` (Boolean) If `true`, the original log is retained after parsing and stored under the `plaintext_log` key. Defaults to `false`.
- `mode` (String) Mode that controls how the parser matches incoming plaintext logs.

<a id="nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `plaintext_parser.parser`

Required:

- `parser_type` (String) Type of parser to apply. Determines which of `regex_parser`, `key_value_parser`, or `grok_parser` must be set.

Optional:

- `grok_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Grok parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `GROK`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser--grok_parser))
- `key_value_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Key/value parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `KEY_VALUE`. Duplicate keys keep the first occurrence. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser--key_value_parser))
- `regex_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Regex parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `REGEX`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser--regex_parser))

<a id="nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser--grok_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `plaintext_parser.parser.grok_parser`

Required:

- `pattern` (String) Grok pattern to apply. Named capture groups become named fields in the extracted log.


<a id="nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser--key_value_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `plaintext_parser.parser.key_value_parser`

Required:

- `delimiter` (String) String used to split the input into individual key/value pairs.
- `pair_separator` (String) String used to split each pair into a key and value.

Optional:

- `trim_set` (String) Unicode code points to trim from the beginning and end of each key and value.


<a id="nestedblock--plaintext_parser--parser--regex_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `plaintext_parser.parser.regex_parser`

Required:

- `regex` (String) RE2 regular expression pattern. Named capturing groups become named fields in the extracted log.
